channel.title: Tech Layoffs on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Tech Layoffs on MinWeb

item.title: Why Your Survival Shouldn’t Be Tied to Your Paycheck
item.description:
If you’ve ever felt like your employer holds your entire life in their hands, you aren’t being paranoid. You’re just observing how the American workforce actually functions.

In late March 2026, the gaming industry faced a story that perfectly captured the darkest side of this reality. Mike Prinke, a programmer who dedicated nearly seven years to Epic Games, was caught in a massive layoff of 1,000 employees. For most people, a layoff is a massive financial headache and a scramble to update a resume. For Prinke, who was battling terminal brain cancer, it was a catastrophe.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Job-Linked Benefits: A Tragic Case Study
item.description:
If you’ve ever scrolled through social media and felt that sudden, cold pit in your stomach, you know exactly what I’m talking about. It’s that moment when a headline cuts through the noise and forces you to confront a terrifying reality: the systems we rely on to keep us safe are much more fragile than we admit.

In March 2026, that reality hit home when Epic Games—the powerhouse behind Fortnite—slashed its workforce by 1,000 employees. Among them was Mike Prinke, a senior programmer who had been with the company for seven years. But one detail turned a standard corporate downsizing story into a national firestorm: Mike was battling terminal brain cancer.

item.title: Why Tim Sweeney’s Comments on Epic Layoffs Sparked Backlash
item.description:
If you’ve spent any time on social media lately, you’ve seen the “corporate layoff announcement.” It’s a predictable script: a CEO expresses deep regret, claims the decision was “incredibly difficult,” and insists that the departing employees were “world-class talent.”

But on March 25, 2026, Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney went a step further. After announcing that Epic would be laying off over 1,000 employees—roughly 20% of the company—Sweeney took to X to declare: “In the coming days, employers will see a stream of resumes of once-in-a-lifetime quality folks.”
